plan.topic: Validate codex-nexus invocation-map.yml usage against nexus-core consumer docs and migration guidance

issues.title: Determine whether codex-nexus invocation-map.yml matches both nexus-core invocation semantics and the official Codex skill/subagent/tool surface
issues.decision: Selected approach: experiment-first validation against the locally installed Codex CLI (version 0.121.0 observed on April 16, 2026) before changing codex-nexus mappings. Reason: official Codex docs clearly ground skills and subagents at a high level, but the authoritative evidence for the exact runtime surfaces relevant here is the local Codex runtime itself, which already exposes strong signals for `spawn_agent` and `request_user_input`. Rejected alternative: continue docs-only investigation before probing. Reason dismissed: the public docs are incomplete for these surfaces, while local binary strings and session/runtime evidence are sufficient to justify direct validation now. Constraint derived from this decision: treat `task_register` as unresolved until a native Codex progress/TUI surface is experimentally confirmed; do not keep mapping it to persistent `nx_task_*` state changes without validation.

plan.research_summary: Local research: codex-nexus reads invocation-map.yml in scripts/generate-from-nexus-core.lib.mjs and uses it only at code-generation time to expand nexus-core body.md macros into Codex-specific prose. The current file defines entries for skill_activation, subagent_spawn, task_register, and user_question. nexus-core docs describe vocabulary/invocations.yml as a harness-local semantic primitive registry; consumer-implementation-guide.md says consumers maintain their own local map from these primitives to concrete syntax and load skill/agent body.md into the LLM context. Installed package v0.10.0 omits CONSUMING.md/MIGRATIONS from node_modules, but upstream GitHub docs confirm the upgrade protocol reads CHANGELOG, migration docs, and semver policy from the repo tag. Upstream CONSUMING.md points consumers to docs/consumer-implementation-guide.md for a new consumer build. CHANGELOG v0.10.0 and migration guidance indicate task decomposition/runtime dispatch changes are consumer-local, while canonical assets remain neutral. Relevant implication: invocation-map.yml is consumer-owned glue, but the docs frame it as mapping semantic primitives to harness syntax/behavior, not as a canonical file from nexus-core. No prior project memory/rules/history entries were found on this topic.

plan.topic: Align codex-nexus session state, tool-log semantics, and subagent capability restrictions with nexus-core

issues.title: Reset session-scoped state files on session start and stop according to nexus-core lifecycle
issues.decision: Selected approach: overwrite session-scoped state on SessionStart and clean up on Stop, while aligning paths/schema to nexus-core. Reason: ensureFile preserves stale state and the current tracker/log locations do not match nexus-core lifecycle expectations. Rejected alternative: keep append-only session files and only clear them opportunistically. Dismissed because it leaves stale tracker/log state across sessions and still violates nexus-core path/schema requirements.

issues.title: Record only real file-modification events in tool-log and use them to populate subagent files_touched
issues.decision: Selected approach: stop logging raw Bash commands and instead derive tool-log entries from transcript file-edit events, recording only concrete file modifications plus result status and touched paths. Reason: Codex hooks expose Bash only, so command logging is a poor proxy; rollout transcripts provide authoritative patch/file-change events. Rejected alternative: regex-classify mutating Bash commands inline. Dismissed because it still logs shell activity rather than actual file-modification tools and misses structured apply_patch/function-call edits.

issues.title: Restrict subagent tool access according to nexus-core capabilities using Codex-native role config and Nexus MCP guards
issues.decision: Selected approach: enforce nexus-core capabilities with role-level Codex config overrides and Nexus MCP guards. For roles with no_file_edit, use read-only sandbox and disable apply_patch; for roles with no_task_create/no_task_update, disable corresponding nx MCP tools and reject guarded calls server-side. Reason: local Codex runtime supports role config layers, include_apply_patch_tool, sandbox_mode, and MCP server tool filters, which is the strongest available native restriction surface. Rejected alternative: rely on prompt-only instructions. Dismissed because nexus-core explicitly expects capability restrictions to be applied by the consumer, not merely described.

plan.research_summary: Current codex-native hook initializes state with ensureFile, so agent-tracker and tool-log persist across sessions instead of resetting. createNexusPaths currently places tool-log under .nexus/state/codex-nexus/tool-log.jsonl, while nexus-core consumer docs require session-scoped tool-log at .nexus/state/tool-log.jsonl and agent-tracker at .nexus/state/{harness-id}/agent-tracker.json as an array. Current agent-tracker schema is an object with harness_id/started_at/invocations, which conflicts with nexus-core docs and schema. PostToolUse currently appends every Bash command, but Codex hook runtime only exposes PreToolUse/PostToolUse for Bash commands; it does not expose generic tool names there. However rollout transcripts include concrete file-edit events: custom_tool_call apply_patch plus patch_apply_end changes with touched file paths. Codex role config supports full config-layer overrides for spawned agents, including sandbox_mode, include_apply_patch_tool, web_search, and mcp_servers.<server>.disabled_tools/enabled_tools. nexus-core manifest/meta define capabilities per role: engineer/writer=no_task_create; researcher/reviewer/tester=no_file_edit+no_task_create; architect/designer/postdoc/strategist=no_file_edit+no_task_create+no_task_update. Therefore the viable implementation is: reset session files at SessionStart, delete tracker at Stop, source tool-log entries from transcript-derived file-edit events only, update files_touched from those entries on subagent completion, and encode role-based restrictions via native agent TOML config plus MCP-side guards for Nexus task mutations.

tasks.title: Reset session-scoped Nexus state and align tracker/log locations
tasks.context: Target files are expected under src/hooks/, src/shared/, src/config/, and tests that cover hook lifecycle/state files. Current implementation preserves agent-tracker and tool-log across sessions, stores tool-log under .nexus/state/codex-nexus/, and writes agent-tracker as an object instead of the nexus-core array schema.
tasks.approach: Update path/state helpers so SessionStart overwrites session-scoped tracker/log files with fresh defaults, places tool-log at .nexus/state/tool-log.jsonl, and makes Stop remove agent-tracker while keeping tool-log/session history behavior aligned with nexus-core docs. Update hook lifecycle tests to assert reset/delete semantics and schema shape.
tasks.acceptance: SessionStart recreates a fresh empty agent-tracker array and a fresh empty tool-log file every session. agent-tracker is stored at .nexus/state/codex-nexus/agent-tracker.json with a top-level array schema. Stop removes agent-tracker when the session can end cleanly.
tasks.risk: Changing session-scoped file layout can break existing tests or local assumptions if helper paths and fixtures are not updated together.

tasks.title: Log only real file-modification events and propagate files_touched
tasks.context: Codex hook runtime exposes Bash-only PreToolUse/PostToolUse events, so current command logging is a weak proxy. Rollout transcripts contain structured apply_patch and patch_apply_end events with changed file paths. The tracker should record files_touched for completed subagents using authoritative file-edit evidence rather than shell command strings.
tasks.approach: Introduce transcript parsing for file-edit events, append tool-log entries only for concrete file modifications with tool name/file path/status, ignore plain Bash command activity, and use accumulated tool-log entries to populate files_touched when a subagent session completes. Add focused tests using transcript fixtures.
tasks.acceptance: tool-log stays empty for non-edit Bash activity. file-edit transcript events append entries with tool name, status, and touched file path(s). Completed subagent tracker entries include files_touched derived from logged edits.
tasks.risk: Transcript parsing must tolerate missing or partial rollout lines without corrupting state or crashing the hook.

tasks.title: Verify session lifecycle, edit logging, and capability enforcement
tasks.context: Verification should focus on the changed hook lifecycle behavior, transcript-derived tool-log/files_touched behavior, generated agent role config restrictions, and Nexus task guard paths.
tasks.approach: Run targeted automated tests covering hook lifecycle/state files, transcript-based edit logging, native agent config generation, and task mutation guards. Extend or add tests where needed before running the focused suite and then the repo test command if time allows.
tasks.acceptance: Focused tests covering the changed behavior pass, and any broader suite run used for verification completes without regressions attributable to these changes.
tasks.risk: Some existing tests may assume the old state-file layout and require coordinated fixture updates before meaningful verification can run.

tasks.title: Apply nexus-core capability restrictions to spawned native agents
tasks.context: Local Codex runtime supports role config layers for spawned agents, including sandbox_mode, include_apply_patch_tool, web_search, and MCP server tool filters. nexus-core assigns role capabilities such as no_file_edit, no_task_create, and no_task_update, but codex-nexus currently emits agent TOML without any real tool restrictions and only guards nx_task_close for subagents.
tasks.approach: Encode nexus-core capabilities in local agent definitions/native agent TOML generation, apply read-only sandbox and apply_patch disablement for no_file_edit roles, disable nx MCP task mutation tools per capability, and add server-side guards for disallowed Nexus task mutations from subagents. Add tests for generated agent config and task guard behavior.
tasks.acceptance: Generated native agent configs reflect nexus-core capability restrictions for each role. Roles with no_file_edit cannot receive apply_patch and run in read-only sandbox. Roles with no_task_create/no_task_update cannot invoke the corresponding nx task mutation tools, with explicit errors when attempted.
tasks.risk: Codex role config can restrict some surfaces natively but not every built-in tool, so runtime guards must be kept consistent with config-layer restrictions.

plan.topic: Fix tool-log path and transcript logging

issues.title: tool-log를 harness state 디렉터리로 옮기고 실환경 transcript에서도 로그가 기록되도록 수정한다.
issues.decision: Canonical tool-log path is `.nexus/state/codex-nexus/tool-log.jsonl`, colocated with `agent-tracker.json`. This follows the user’s instruction and the namespace-oriented outputs-contract examples; Stop-time transcript replay is retained only as fallback, while primary logging should happen on `PostToolUse`.

issues.title: 현재 구현을 nexus-core 규칙/생성 소스와 대조해 위배 사항이 있으면 수정하고 검증한다.

plan.research_summary: 현재 구현은 AGENT_TRACKER_FILE을 `.nexus/state/codex-nexus/agent-tracker.json`에 두지만 TOOL_LOG_FILE은 `.nexus/state/tool-log.jsonl`에 둔다. 실제 subagent transcript에는 테스트 더블이 가정한 `event_msg.patch_apply_end`뿐 아니라 실환경의 `response_item.custom_tool_call apply_patch`와 `response_item.function_call exec_command`가 존재한다. 현재 로직은 patch_apply_end만 읽어서 실환경 tool-log가 비어 있다. 추가로 완료 후 nexus-core 규칙 위배 여부를 점검해야 한다.

tasks.context: 현재 `src/shared/paths.ts`는 `AGENT_TRACKER_FILE`을 HARNESS_STATE_ROOT에 두지만 `TOOL_LOG_FILE`은 STATE_ROOT에 둔다. `src/shared/codex-session.ts`는 `event_msg.patch_apply_end`만 읽어 apply_patch 항목만 생성한다. 실 transcript에는 `response_item.custom_tool_call`과 `response_item.function_call` 형식이 존재한다. 관련 검증은 `test/hook.test.ts`에 있다.

tasks.title: Align hook timing and tracker schema with nexus-core guidance
tasks.context: Reviewer found that `src/hooks/codex-native-hook.ts` logs via transcript replay on `Stop` instead of appending during `PostToolUse`, and `src/config/codex-hooks.ts` only wires `PostToolUse` for Bash. Additionally, local inspection shows `src/shared/state.ts` writes non-schema fields to `agent-tracker.json`, while nexus-core `conformance/state-schemas/agent-tracker.schema.json` allows only `harness_id`, `agent_name`, `agent_id`, `started_at`, `last_resumed_at`, `resume_count`, `status`, `stopped_at`, `last_message`, `files_touched`. `src/mcp/tools/plan.ts` currently reads non-schema tracker fields like `session_id` and `last_summary`.
tasks.approach: Update hook wiring to receive relevant PostToolUse events, append tool-log entries at PostToolUse for `apply_patch` while keeping Stop-time transcript replay as fallback, make `agent-tracker.json` schema-conformant, and adapt plan-tool consumers/tests to use schema-valid fields (`agent_id`, `last_message`, etc.) instead of removed custom fields.
tasks.acceptance: 1) tool-log appends on PostToolUse for apply_patch. 2) Stop replay remains idempotent fallback. 3) agent-tracker entries are valid against nexus-core schema shape. 4) affected tests pass.
tasks.risk: 중간. Hook payload shape와 tracker consumer 변경이 함께 엮여 있어 테스트 보강이 필요하다.

plan.topic: Fix malformed custom agent role definitions caused by incomplete MCP server config in generated .codex/agents/*.toml

issues.title: Determine the minimal generator change that makes generated custom agent files valid for Codex 0.121.0 while preserving per-agent nx tool restrictions.
issues.decision: Selected approach: update the native agent TOML generator so every emitted `[mcp_servers.nx]` block includes the same stdio transport used by project config (`command = "bun"` plus `args = [<packageRoot>/dist/mcp/server.js]`) and keep `disabled_tools` in that same block. Reason: this matches the Codex config schema for MCP server definitions and directly addresses the observed `invalid transport` parse failure for standalone agent files. Rejected alternative: remove the `mcp_servers.nx` block entirely from custom agent files. Reason dismissed: it would avoid the parse error but would also silently drop per-agent nx tool restrictions that this project intentionally relies on. Rejected alternative: keep emitting only `disabled_tools` and treat the error as a Codex-only bug. Reason dismissed: even if Codex has a parser inconsistency, generating a complete MCP definition is valid per current docs and gives a robust workaround without waiting on upstream fixes.

plan.research_summary: Investigated the local repository generator, local Codex CLI 0.121.0, and current OpenAI Codex docs on 2026-04-17. Generated custom agent files currently emit `[mcp_servers.nx]` with only `disabled_tools`, for example `.codex/agents/architect.toml`. OpenAI docs say custom agent files may include `mcp_servers`, and the config reference/schema define `mcp_servers.<id>` with transport fields such as `command` or `url` plus optional `disabled_tools`. The local Codex runtime reports `Ignoring malformed agent role definition ... invalid transport`, which indicates the standalone agent-role parser is requiring a valid MCP transport when an `mcp_servers.<id>` block is present. Therefore the likely fix is to generate a complete `mcp_servers.nx` definition including the existing stdio transport (`command = "bun"`, `args = [<dist/mcp/server.js>]`) alongside `disabled_tools`, then update tests accordingly.

tasks.title: Update native agent TOML generation to emit complete nx MCP server definitions

Plan continuity should prefer how_agent_ids recorded in plan.json, then tracker continuity; run continuity should prefer explicit owner_agent_id / owner_reuse_policy from tasks, then tracker-based reuse checks using files_touched for bounded tiers.

plan.topic: Align codex-nexus with @moreih29/nexus-core v0.12.0 from the current v0.10.0 baseline

issues.title: Bump the consumer baseline from @moreih29/nexus-core 0.10.0 to 0.12.0 and verify generated assets remain in sync
issues.decision: Selected approach: Option C — bump @moreih29/nexus-core from 0.10.0 to 0.12.0, regenerate consumer-owned outputs, and add/execute an explicit verification gate rather than treating this as a lockfile-only update. Reason: upstream comparison shows generated skill bodies are expected to stay mostly stable, but codex-nexus is a contract-sensitive consumer and should prove that stability with regeneration and verification rather than assume it. Rejected alternative A: minimal dependency/lockfile bump only. Dismissed because it leaves no evidence that consumer-owned generated assets and contract assumptions still match the new nexus-core baseline. Rejected alternative B: dependency bump plus regeneration/diff inspection without a stronger verification gate. Dismissed because it improves confidence but still under-specifies upgrade safety for future schema/fixture drift and makes the consumer's compliance posture weaker than necessary.

issues.title: Add the nexus-core upgrade protocol to AGENTS surfaces so future version bumps follow the official CONSUMING.md flow
issues.decision: Selected approach: Option B — add the official @moreih29/nexus-core upgrade protocol block to both the repository root AGENTS.md and the install-time templates/AGENTS.md surface. Reason: the root AGENTS.md governs work in this repo today, while templates/AGENTS.md governs the AGENTS section written into downstream installs; keeping both in sync ensures the current consumer repo and newly installed consumer surfaces follow the same CONSUMING.md-prescribed upgrade flow. Rejected alternative A: add the protocol only to templates/AGENTS.md. Dismissed because it would fix future installed outputs but leave this repository's own active agent-memory surface behind the official protocol. Rejected alternative C: move the protocol to a different surface instead of AGENTS. Dismissed because CONSUMING.md explicitly instructs consumer repos to place the one-time setup in an agent-memory file such as AGENTS.md, so a non-AGENTS-only placement would weaken compliance and discoverability.

issues.title: Add a validate:conformance entrypoint and fixture coverage checks aligned with nexus-core v0.12.0 conformance expectations
issues.decision: Selected approach: Option B — add a dedicated validate:conformance entrypoint and include it in the default check path, without also forcing it into the broader test command. Reason: this makes conformance coverage part of the normal static verification loop while keeping the heavier build-and-test path focused on runtime behavior. Rejected alternative A: add only a standalone script. Dismissed because an opt-in command would be easy to forget and would not reliably protect against schema/fixture drift. Rejected alternative C: force conformance validation through both check and test. Dismissed because it increases verification surface and runtime cost without enough additional value; conformance coverage belongs in the contract/static validation lane more than in the runtime test lane.

plan.research_summary: Current codex-nexus is pinned to @moreih29/nexus-core ^0.10.0 in package.json and bun.lock, and node_modules currently contains 0.10.0. npm latest and the upstream GitHub tags both show 0.12.0 as the latest release on 2026-04-17. Upstream CHANGELOG and MIGRATIONS/v0_11_to_v0_12.md explicitly say codex-nexus may skip v0.11.0 and upgrade directly from v0.10.x to v0.12.0; the withdrawn v0.11.0 obligation was creating harness-content/nexus-hook-mapping.md and accepting the nexus_hook_mapping token. Local code already does not implement nexus_hook_mapping and only appends resume_invocation harness docs, so no hook-mapping dead-code removal is needed. Direct package comparison between local 0.10.0 and packed 0.12.0 shows skills/nx-run body/meta, skills/nx-plan body/meta, and vocabulary/invocations.yml are unchanged; generated skill output is therefore expected to stay stable after a version bump. The meaningful upstream diffs are docs/consumer-implementation-guide.md changes, manifest version/ordering metadata, package metadata, validate script internals, and two added lifecycle fixtures: conformance/lifecycle/session-end.json and conformance/lifecycle/memory-access-record.json. Local repo currently lacks a validate:conformance script or any explicit invocation of nexus-validate-conformance, and ripgrep found no local conformance fixture coverage checks beyond upstream files sitting in node_modules. Current AGENTS.md and templates/AGENTS.md also do not include the one-time nexus-core upgrade protocol block that CONSUMING.md instructs consumer repos to place in agent memory. Therefore the required consumer-side work is: update the devDependency/lockfile to 0.12.0, add the official upgrade-protocol memory to AGENTS surfaces, and establish a first-class conformance validation command that covers the newly added lifecycle fixtures and future schema-field drift.

tasks.title: Upgrade nexus-core baseline and regenerate Nexus-derived outputs
tasks.context: task_exception=generated_artifacts; includes package.json/bun.lock baseline changes plus any generated prompts/skills/templates that must be regenerated under the new manifest.
tasks.approach: Bump @moreih29/nexus-core to ^0.12.0, refresh the lockfile/install state, rerun the generation pipeline, and inspect consumer-owned generated outputs for drift while keeping the existing invocation-map semantics intact.
tasks.acceptance: package.json and bun.lock reference @moreih29/nexus-core ^0.12.0; generation succeeds against 0.12.0; generated outputs in the repo are either intentionally updated or confirmed unchanged except for expected version metadata.
tasks.risk: Upstream manifest and contract-document changes may expose latent assumptions in the generator or install pipeline even when skill bodies are unchanged.

tasks.title: Add nexus-core upgrade protocol to AGENTS surfaces
tasks.context: task_exception=docs_only.coherent; update the root AGENTS.md and templates/AGENTS.md as one coherent documentation unit so repo-local and install-time AGENTS surfaces stay synchronized.
tasks.approach: Add the official @moreih29/nexus-core upgrade protocol block from CONSUMING.md to both AGENTS surfaces with the same wording and explicit read order, keeping the surrounding Nexus orchestration guidance intact.
tasks.acceptance: Both AGENTS.md and templates/AGENTS.md contain the same upgrade protocol guidance, and the protocol instructs agents to detect version changes and read manifest.json, CONSUMING.md, CHANGELOG, MIGRATIONS, and semver-policy in the prescribed order.
tasks.risk: Duplicated protocol text across two AGENTS surfaces can drift unless both files are updated together.

tasks.title: Review AGENTS upgrade protocol against nexus-core CONSUMING.md

tasks.approach: Compare the inserted protocol block in the root and template AGENTS surfaces against the official CONSUMING.md requirements and confirm the two files remain synchronized and discoverable.
tasks.acceptance: Review confirms that both AGENTS surfaces faithfully capture the required upgrade protocol steps and ordering from CONSUMING.md, or identifies exact wording gaps that must be corrected.
tasks.risk: Minor wording drift from the official protocol can weaken future agent compliance even when the general intent is correct.

tasks.title: Add conformance validation command to the default check path
tasks.context: Wire a validate:conformance entrypoint into package scripts and the default static verification lane without also forcing it into the broader runtime-test path.
tasks.approach: Expose a validate:conformance script that runs nexus-core conformance coverage on the upgraded 0.12.0 baseline, then include it in the existing check pipeline alongside generation and typechecking; update any directly affected tests or command expectations.
tasks.acceptance: package.json exposes bun run validate:conformance; bun run check invokes conformance validation as part of its default path; any affected tests or command-surface expectations are updated accordingly.
tasks.risk: The stricter 0.12.0 lifecycle fixture coverage may surface missing local conformance coverage and require follow-up script or fixture adjustments.

tasks.title: Verify the 0.12.0 upgrade and conformance gate
tasks.context: Focused verification task for the upgraded nexus-core baseline, regenerated outputs, and the new conformance-validation path.
tasks.approach: Run the relevant verification commands on the upgraded baseline — including generation, validate:conformance, check, and the most relevant automated tests — and capture whether the new contract/static validation lane works end-to-end.
tasks.acceptance: Verification produces concrete evidence that the 0.12.0 baseline, regenerated outputs, and validate:conformance-integrated check path work together, or reports exact failing commands and failure surfaces.
tasks.risk: Broader test failures unrelated to the nexus-core upgrade may obscure whether remaining problems are introduced regressions or pre-existing issues.

tasks.title: Restrict stop hook gating to run mode and clear run marker on cycle close
tasks.context: Update hook lifecycle so plan-only sessions do not trigger stop blocking. Use a run-mode marker set by [run], consulted by Stop, and cleared on SessionStart/task_close. Cover with focused hook/task tests.
tasks.approach: Introduce a harness state file representing an active run cycle, set it from the UserPromptSubmit [run] path, gate Stop only when that file exists, and clear it on session reset and nx_task_close. Add regression tests for plan-vs-run stop behavior.
tasks.acceptance: Stop does not block during plan-only state that has tasks.json but no active run marker. Stop blocks during run-mode sessions with pending/in-progress tasks or all-complete-but-not-archived tasks. nx_task_close clears the run marker so a clean stop no longer blocks.
tasks.risk: If the run marker is not consistently cleared, stop could remain sticky across sessions or fail to protect active run cycles.

plan.topic: Migrate codex-nexus to a nexus-core-backed wrapper architecture

issues.title: Replace legacy manifest-driven generation and package layout with upstream nexus-core Codex scaffold consumption
issues.decision: Adopt upstream nexus-core Codex scaffold outputs as the package asset source of truth. Remove legacy manifest/meta-driven generation and repoint build/package scripts to sync-based generated outputs.

how_summary.architect: The old manifest-based consumer contract is gone upstream. Build-time sync against the Codex harness output is the stable boundary and avoids reimplementing agent/skill packaging locally.

issues.title: Shift install/runtime ownership so core assets and MCP are consumed directly while codex-nexus stays a thin Codex wrapper
issues.decision: Keep codex-nexus as a thin Codex wrapper: install CLI, config merge, doctor checks, and any explicit Codex-only additions remain local, but shared core assets and shared MCP runtime should be sourced from nexus-core wherever possible.

how_summary.architect: Wrapper responsibilities should be limited to Codex-specific installation and integration seams. Shared orchestration assets and shared runtime should be delegated to nexus-core to reduce drift.

issues.title: Update documentation, tests, and developer workflow to match the new wrapper contract
issues.decision: Refresh README/help/tests around the new wrapper contract and delete obsolete upgrade/conformance guidance that assumes the pre-0.13 manifest layout.

how_summary.writer: Docs and tests need to describe codex-nexus as a Codex-facing wrapper over nexus-core, not as an independent implementation of shared Nexus runtime behavior.

plan.research_summary: Reviewed local codex-nexus implementation and latest upstream nexus-core documentation and source. Current codex-nexus depends on removed manifest-era contracts from nexus-core 0.10.x, while upstream 0.13.0 now ships assets/, hooks runtime, a nexus-mcp server, and Codex scaffold outputs via sync --harness=codex. Local runtime duplicates upstream MCP, prompt generation, agent TOML generation, and parts of hook behavior. Recommended direction is to make nexus-core the source of truth for generated Codex assets and shared runtime surfaces, while codex-nexus keeps Codex-specific install, merge, doctor, and any explicit wrapper-only extensions.

plan.topic: Consume nexus-core v0.17.0 runtimes in codex-nexus

issues.title: Upgrade codex-nexus to nexus-core v0.17.0 and resync managed Codex outputs
issues.decision: Selected: upgrade codex-nexus to @moreih29/nexus-core v0.17.0 and resync all managed Codex assets before changing wrapper runtime wiring. Rejected alternative: refactor wrapper runtime first on top of 0.16.2 because the ownership contract and shipped Codex hook outputs changed in 0.17.0, so delaying the upgrade would force work against an obsolete package layout.

issues.title: Switch Codex install/runtime wiring from wrapper-built MCP/hooks to nexus-core-provided runtimes
issues.decision: Selected: stop building wrapper-local MCP and hook runtimes, and instead point install/runtime wiring at nexus-core-provided runtimes from the installed dependency tree. Rejected alternative: keep wrapper-built runtimes and only sync more core files, because that preserves duplicate ownership and contradicts the v0.17.0 contract that core owns runtime exports while the wrapper owns merge and distribution seams.

issues.title: Delete obsolete wrapper runtime code and align tests/docs with the reduced ownership model
issues.decision: Selected: remove obsolete wrapper runtime source/tests after runtime consumers are switched, then rewrite docs/tests around integration expectations. Rejected alternative: leave dead wrapper runtime files in place for compatibility because the user explicitly requested wrapper reduction and stale runtime code would continue to confuse ownership boundaries and future upgrades.

plan.research_summary: Current codex-nexus publishes its own runtime artifacts: package.json ships dist/, scripts/build.mjs bundles src/mcp/server.ts and src/hooks/codex-native-hook.ts, and install writes config/hooks that point to wrapper dist paths. sync-from-nexus-core only copies agents/plugin/prompts/install fragments, not core hook bundles or runtime JS. npm package dry-run confirms the tarball contains dist/mcp/server.js and dist/hooks/codex-native-hook.js but no core Codex hook files. nexus-core v0.17.0 package exposes runtime bin nexus-mcp at dist/src/mcp/server.js and ships Codex prebuilt outputs under dist/codex including hooks/hooks.json, dist/codex/dist/hooks/*.js, install fragments, agents, prompts, and plugin skills. The harness contract states core owns runtime exports (hooks manifests and mcp server) while the wrapper owns install-time merge logic, OS path resolution, and distribution. Therefore the wrapper should upgrade to v0.17.0, consume core runtime files or dependency paths at install/build time, delete wrapper-owned MCP/hook runtime implementations that duplicate core, and reduce tests/docs to verify integration rather than custom runtime behavior.

tasks.title: Upgrade dependency and resync managed Codex outputs
tasks.approach: Bump @moreih29/nexus-core to ^0.17.0, inspect the resynced Codex outputs, and update wrapper packaging assumptions to match the new upstream layout before touching runtime wiring.
tasks.acceptance: package.json and lockfile resolve to nexus-core v0.17.0; sync:core succeeds; repo-managed agents/plugin/prompts/install assets reflect v0.17.0 outputs; no wrapper code still assumes the old upstream layout.

tasks.title: Consume core MCP and hook runtimes in install/build paths
tasks.approach: Replace wrapper MCP/hook build outputs and install merge logic with core runtime references or copied core-managed files, keeping wrapper ownership limited to install-time path resolution and merge behavior.
tasks.acceptance: generated config.toml and hooks.json reference nexus-core-provided runtimes; wrapper packaging/build no longer ships or depends on local MCP/hook runtime implementations; install behavior remains functional for user and project scopes.

tasks.title: Delete obsolete wrapper runtime code and realign tests/docs
tasks.approach: Delete superseded src/mcp, src/hooks, and related state/runtime tests only after new integration paths are in place; then rewrite tests and docs around install/runtime consumption of nexus-core exports.
tasks.acceptance: obsolete wrapper runtime files are removed; tests cover integration with core runtimes rather than local implementations; docs explain the new ownership boundary; check/test pass.

tasks.title: Remove tmp and release runtime-reduction update
tasks.acceptance: tmp directory removed; package/VERSION/plugin manifest aligned to a new unpublished version; checks/tests pass; changes committed; PR merged to main; release tag pushed; publish workflow succeeds or a concrete blocker is recorded.
